vision.title: Vision
vision.label: Our Purpose
vision.text: To emerge as a global center of excellence in agricultural education, research, and extension, dedicated to ensuring food security, sustainable farming, and empowering rural economies.

mission.label: Our Goal
points: Deliver ICAR-aligned, rigorous academic programs combining theoretical agriculture with intensive field-level training.
Foster research in sustainable farming, precision agriculture, and modern horticulture techniques.
Equip students with the entrepreneurial skills required to launch successful Agri-Tech startups.
Facilitate extensive 'Rural Agricultural Work Experience' (RAWE) to connect students with grassroots farming communities.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture is a highly prestigious 4-year professional degree. Unlike general B.Sc. programs, an 'Honours' degree in agriculture is specifically demanded by government agencies and top corporates. It is a deeply technical and scientific course that covers everything from soil health and crop genetics to farm machinery and agricultural economics.

Our curriculum does not restrict students to classrooms. A massive portion of the degree is completed out on the 50-acre instructional farm. Students get their hands dirty by actively growing crops, operating tractors, testing soil in labs, and finally spending their 7th and 8th semesters living in villages to educate real farmers through the RAWE program.

scope.body: The agricultural sector is the backbone of the Indian economy, and professional graduates are in extremely high demand. This degree is the primary gateway to secure, high-paying government jobs like Agriculture Field Officer (AFO in Banks), Agriculture Development Officer (ADO), and Forest Officer. In the private sector, graduates are recruited by seed companies (Mahyco), FMCG giants (ITC, Amul), fertilizer companies (UPL), and modern Agri-Tech startups. It is also the necessary stepping stone for pursuing an M.Sc. in specialized agricultural branches.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in Government Banks, FMCG, and Agri-Tech

curriculum.subtitle: An ICAR-aligned curriculum blending lab science with field practice
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum is meticulously structured, starting with basic allied sciences and moving into core agricultural technologies and economics.
curriculum.outroNote: The entire final year is dedicated to the Rural Agricultural Work Experience (RAWE) and Agro-Industrial Attachment (AIA).

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 4-year B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture program spans 8 semesters. Years 1 to 3 involve rigorous classroom theory paired equally with practical farm work. Students learn how to prepare soil, plant seeds, manage pests, and calculate farm profits. Year 4 is completely stripped of regular classes, focusing 100% on rural immersion (RAWE) and industrial training.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Foundation of Agricultural Sciences
accordionSections.content: Fundamentals of Agronomy, Fundamentals of Soil Science, Fundamentals of Plant Biochemistry, Comprehension & Communication Skills, Introduction to Forestry, Introductory Animal Husbandry.

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Crop & Pest Management
accordionSections.content: Crop Production Technology (Kharif & Rabi), Fundamentals of Entomology, Fundamentals of Plant Pathology, Agricultural Microbiology, Farm Machinery and Power, Principles of Seed Technology.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: Advanced Farming & Economics
accordionSections.content: Principles of Integrated Pest and Disease Management, Manures & Fertilizers, Diseases of Field and Horticultural Crops, Agricultural Marketing Trade & Prices, Protected Cultivation (Polyhouse), Entrepreneurship Development.

accordionSections.title: Year-4: RAWE & Industrial Attachment
accordionSections.content: Rural Agricultural Work Experience (RAWE) - Village Attachment, Agro-Industrial Attachment (AIA) - Working with Seed/Fertilizer companies, Plant Clinic Postings.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the Agriculture programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. You MUST be from the Science stream (PCM or PCB) OR have studied Agriculture as a main subject in 12th.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can Commerce/Arts students apply?

items.a: It is a 4-year degree program divided into 8 semesters. The tuition fee is ₹35,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: A general B.Sc. Agriculture is 3 years and lacks strict practical guidelines. The 'Honours' degree is a 4-year professional course mandated by ICAR. Government jobs (like AFO) heavily prefer or strictly require the 4-Year Honours degree.
items.q: Why should I choose an 'Honours' degree over a regular B.Sc.?

items.a: Graduates are highly targeted for the Bank AFO (Agriculture Field Officer) role. They are also hired by seed companies (Mahyco), fertilizer corporations (UPL), and FMCG giants (ITC). Many students also clear state-level Agriculture Development Officer (ADO) exams.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture?

items.a: Yes! The entire 7th semester is dedicated to RAWE, where you live and work in rural villages. The 8th semester is an Industrial Attachment where you intern at seed or fertilizer companies. There are NO regular classroom lectures in the final year.
items.q: Is field work or an internship mandatory?

items.a: Yes, we actively provide special classes and mentoring to help our students clear competitive exams like Bank AFO, Nabard Grade A, and State ADO.
items.q: Does the university help in Bank AFO preparation?

items.a: Yes, completing this 4-Year Honours degree makes you fully eligible to appear for the ICAR AIEEA (PG) exams to pursue a Master's (M.Sc.) in specific agricultural branches.
items.q: Can I pursue a Master's degree after this?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from a science student to an elite Agricultural Expert.

years.contentDesc: The first year builds your foundational knowledge in core sciences, introducing you to agronomy, soil chemistry, and the basics of farming.
Deepen your field knowledge by learning how to protect crops. Master entomology (insects), plant pathology (diseases), and farm machinery.
Transition from farming to the business of farming. Learn agricultural economics, marketing, and advanced protected cultivation (Polyhouses).
The final year consists entirely of field work. You will live in villages (RAWE) to teach farmers and intern at corporate seed/fertilizer companies.

list.name: Rahul Verma
list.text: The rigorous practical training and ICAR-aligned syllabus made cracking the IBPS AFO exam a breeze. The faculty support here is incredible.

list.name: Ananya Sharma
list.text: The 6 months I spent in the Agro-Industrial Attachment program directly secured my placement at UPL. The industry exposure is unmatched.

list.name: Siddharth Jain
list.text: The RAWE program changed my life. Understanding real farmer problems pushed me to launch my own startup delivering drone-based crop analysis.

accomplishments.heading: Department's Postgraduate Agricultural Impact

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Sc. Agronomy Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Science (Agri.) in Agronomy is the most foundational and critical postgraduate specialization in agriculture. While a B.Sc. gives a broad overview of all farming aspects, an M.Sc. in Agronomy deeply explores the scientific principles of crop-soil-water interactions to maximize agricultural productivity and sustainability.

The program is heavily research-driven. Students spend massive amounts of time on the university's instructional farms, conducting precise field experiments, analyzing crop data using advanced statistical software, and formulating new farming techniques. The degree culminates in an extensive, field-based Master's Research Thesis.

scope.body: Agronomists are the core scientists driving the agricultural industry. Graduates are highly demanded as Senior Agronomists, Subject Matter Specialists, and Farm Managers in top seed and fertilizer companies (UPL, Mahyco, Bayer). Furthermore, it is the primary qualification to clear the Agricultural Scientist Recruitment Board (ASRB) NET, enabling graduates to become Assistant Professors in Agricultural Universities or Senior Scientists in ICAR research institutes.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in Agri-R&D, Academia, and Corporate Farming

curriculum.subtitle: An ICAR-mandated master's curriculum focusing on field research
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum dives immediately into advanced crop modeling, statistical analysis, and complex weed management techniques.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory, year-long field research experiment resulting in a Master's Thesis must be defended to graduate.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year M.Sc. Agronomy program operates on a 4-semester system. Semesters 1 and 2 involve intense coursework in advanced crop production, soil fertility, and statistical experimental design. Semesters 3 and 4 are completely dedicated to conducting a live field experiment, recording crop data, and writing a comprehensive Master's Thesis.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Modern Concepts in Crop Production, Principles and Practices of Soil Fertility and Nutrient Management, Statistical Methods for Applied Sciences, Minor Course (e.g., Soil Chemistry), Basic Field Research Setup.

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Principles and Practices of Weed Management, Agronomy of Major Cereals and Pulses, Experimental Designs in Agriculture, Minor Course (e.g., Plant Physiology), Research Synopsis Presentation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Dryland Farming and Watershed Management, Agronomy of Oilseed, Fibre and Sugar Crops, Master's Research (Field Experiment Execution & Data Collection), Master's Seminar.

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Master's Research (Data Analysis and Thesis Writing), Final Thesis Submission, Research Paper Publication, Final Viva-Voce Defense.

items.a: Candidates MUST hold a 4-year B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture degree with a minimum of 55% marks. General 3-year B.Sc. (Botany/Zoology) graduates are NOT eligible for this ICAR-aligned course.

items.a: It is a 2-year (4 semester) postgraduate program. The tuition fee is ₹40,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: Agronomy is the foundational science of crop production, focusing on field crops (wheat, rice), soil, and weed management. Horticulture is a different specialization focusing specifically on fruits, vegetables, and flowers.
items.q: What is the difference between Agronomy and Horticulture?

items.a: Yes, conducting an independent, year-long field experiment and submitting a heavily researched Master's Dissertation (Thesis) is a strict mandatory requirement to obtain the degree.

items.a: Graduates are hired as Senior Agronomists, Farm Managers, and R&D Scientists in seed and fertilizer companies (UPL, Bayer, Mahyco). Many clear ASRB NET to become Assistant Professors in agricultural universities.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after M.Sc. Agronomy?

items.a: Yes, our curriculum heavily integrates advanced statistics, specifically teaching you how to use software like 'R' and SPSS to analyze complex crop yield data.
items.q: Will I learn statistical data analysis?

items.a: Yes, completing an M.Sc. in Agronomy is the standard requirement if you wish to clear the ICAR-SRF exam and pursue a Ph.D. in Agricultural Sciences.

accomplishments.heading: Department's Horticultural Impact & Innovation
accomplishments.trustBadge: ✓ Curriculum strictly aligned with ICAR (BSMA) Guidelines

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Sc. Horticulture Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Science (Agri.) in Horticulture is a high-value specialization dealing with the intense cultivation of fruits, vegetables, flowers, spices, and plantation crops. Unlike general agronomy, horticulture focuses heavily on commercial, high-profit crops, aesthetic landscaping, and the crucial post-harvest processing of perishable foods.

Our curriculum heavily leverages the university's advanced Polyhouses and Greenhouses. Students learn climate-controlled farming, hydroponics, genetic breeding of fruits, and advanced orchard management. The second year mandates an independent research thesis where students must develop, test, or optimize a specific horticultural crop variety or farming technique.

scope.body: Horticulture is the most commercially profitable branch of agriculture. Graduates are hired as Horticulture Officers in State Departments, Plantation Managers, and Landscape Architects. Massive demand exists in the FMCG and Food Processing sector (e.g., ITC, Kissan, Tropicana) for experts in post-harvest technology and cold chain management. Furthermore, the technical knowledge gained allows graduates to launch highly profitable Agri-Startups focusing on organic farming, floriculture exports, or hydroponics.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in Commercial Farming, FMCG, and Agri-Startups

curriculum.subtitle: An ICAR-mandated master's curriculum focusing on high-value crops
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum dives into advanced breeding, modern propagation techniques, and the complex engineering of climate-controlled greenhouses.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory, year-long experimental research thesis based in our orchards or polyhouses must be defended to graduate.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year M.Sc. Horticulture program operates on a 4-semester system. Semesters 1 and 2 focus on the advanced propagation, breeding, and production technology of various horticultural crops. Semesters 3 and 4 shift focus to commercial aspects like post-harvest technology, landscaping, and executing an independent Master's Research Thesis.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Production Technology of Warm Season Fruit Crops, Fundamentals of Olericulture (Vegetable Science), Plant Propagation and Nursery Management, Statistical Methods for Applied Sciences, Horticulture Lab Practice I.

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Production Technology of Cool Season Fruit Crops, Breeding of Horticultural Crops, Protected Cultivation (Greenhouse/Polyhouse Tech), Experimental Designs in Agriculture, Research Synopsis Presentation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Commercial Floriculture and Landscaping, Post-Harvest Technology of Horticultural Crops, Spices and Plantation Crops, Master's Research (Data Collection & Field Execution), Master's Seminar.

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Master's Research (Data Analysis and Thesis Writing), Final Thesis Submission, Research Paper Publication, Final Viva-Voce Defense.

description: The School of Agricultural Sciences doesn't operate in closed rooms; our laboratory is the open field. Students spend massive amounts of time on our 50+ acre instructional farms, growing crops, testing soils, and managing dairy and poultry units.

Through the mandatory RAWE (Rural Agricultural Work Experience) program, our students live in villages, understanding grassroots farming problems, and educating local farmers on modern, sustainable, and organic agricultural practices.

list.desc: Massive on-campus agricultural land dedicated purely to student crop production, research, and tractor/machinery training.

list.title: Advanced Soil & Plant Labs
list.desc: State-of-the-art laboratories for Soil Science, Plant Pathology, Entomology, and Genetics equipped with modern diagnostic tools.

list.title: Polyhouse & Greenhouses
list.desc: Climate-controlled structures for teaching advanced protected cultivation and modern horticulture techniques.

list.title: Dairy & Poultry Unit
list.desc: Live animal husbandry units where students learn practical management, breeding, and veterinary basics.

list.name: Rahul Verma
list.text: The rigorous practical training and ICAR-aligned syllabus made cracking the IBPS AFO exam a breeze. The faculty support here is incredible.

list.name: Ananya Sharma
list.text: Conducting my Master's research on the 50-acre university farm gave me the exact practical data I needed to secure a top R&D role in the seed industry.

list.name: Siddharth Jain
list.text: The RAWE program changed my life. Understanding real farmer problems pushed me to launch my own startup delivering drone-based crop analysis.

exploreDepartment.subtitle: Discover our massive farms, research labs, and rural engagement modules
